description: A product manager accumulating decisions, metrics, and roadmap context. Hard questions: paraphrase, multi-hop across decisions, temporal supersession, and abstention on un-decided items.

memories.text: We define an active user as someone who completes at least one core action in a rolling 7-day window, not just a login.
Decision: we cut the onboarding from five steps to three after funnel data showed 60% drop-off on step four.
The Q2 north-star metric is weekly active teams, replacing the Q1 north-star of total signups.
The Q1 north-star metric was total signups.
Enterprise deals require SOC 2 Type II; we scoped the audit for Q3 because two pipeline deals are blocked on it.
Feature flags default off in production and are rolled out by cohort, starting with internal users, then 5% of traffic.
We pushed the mobile app to next year to focus all engineering on the API platform, which three partner products depend on.
Support SLA: first response within 4 business hours for paid plans, 24 hours for free.
Churn is reviewed monthly; the trigger for an intervention is logo churn above 3% in any month.
Pricing experiments run for a full billing cycle before we read results, to avoid mid-cycle bias.

questions.question: If someone just signs in but does nothing, do they count toward our engagement number?
questions.answer: No — an active user must complete at least one core action in a rolling 7-day window, not just log in.

questions.question: What are we measuring success by right now, and what were we measuring before?
questions.answer: Now: weekly active teams (Q2 north-star); before: total signups (Q1 north-star, superseded).

questions.question: Why did we shorten the signup flow?
questions.answer: Funnel data showed 60% drop-off on step four, so onboarding was cut from five steps to three.

questions.question: Why is the native app delayed, and what got the engineering time instead?
questions.answer: The mobile app was pushed to next year to focus engineering on the API platform, which three partner products depend on.

questions.question: A free-tier customer opened a ticket — how fast do we owe them a first reply?
questions.answer: Within 24 hours (paid plans get 4 business hours).

questions.question: What compliance certification is blocking enterprise deals and when is it scheduled?
questions.answer: SOC 2 Type II, with the audit scoped for Q3 (two pipeline deals are blocked on it).

questions.question: At what churn level do we step in?
questions.answer: When logo churn exceeds 3% in any month (reviewed monthly).

questions.question: What's the headcount plan for the design team next quarter?
